judge_results.criterion_text: Mentions Multi-Query Attention (MQA) or Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) as a KV-cache optimization
judge_results.explanation: The report explicitly mentions MQA/GQA (Attention Architecture) as a KV-cache optimization technique under the 'Techniques Compared' section, noting that GQA reduces KV memory by 4-8x.

judge_results.criterion_text: Correctly states that GQA uses fewer KV heads than standard multi-head attention but more than MQA
judge_results.explanation: The report describes MHA as having unique KV projections for every head, MQA as sharing a single KV head, and GQA as assigning one KV head to each group of query heads, explicitly characterizing GQA as a middle ground between MHA and MQA.

judge_results.criterion_text: Discusses trade-offs between memory savings and generation quality
judge_results.explanation: The report explicitly discusses the trade-offs between memory savings and generation quality/perplexity across various techniques, noting that MQA causes moderate-to-high degradation for extreme savings, GQA and FP8 quantization offer high savings with minimal quality loss, and INT4 achieves 4x memory savings at the cost of slight-to-moderate quality degradation.

judge_results.criterion_text: Claims that KV-cache optimization has no impact on output quality
judge_results.explanation: The report acknowledges that KV-cache quantization techniques can impact output quality, noting near-zero loss for FP8 and minor quality degradation for INT4, thereby avoiding the claim that optimization has no impact on quality.

judge_results.criterion_text: Mentions Multi-Query Attention (MQA) or Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) as a KV-cache optimization
judge_results.explanation: The report explicitly discusses both Multi-Query Attention (MQA) and Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) in Section 3 (A & B) and Section 4 as key architectural optimizations to reduce the memory footprint and bandwidth consumption of the KV-cache.

judge_results.criterion_text: Correctly states that GQA uses fewer KV heads than standard multi-head attention but more than MQA
judge_results.explanation: While the report describes the mechanisms of MHA (one KV head per query head), MQA (a single shared KV head), and GQA (grouped query heads sharing KV heads), it never explicitly states that GQA uses fewer KV heads than standard multi-head attention but more than MQA.

judge_results.criterion_text: Discusses trade-offs between memory savings and generation quality
judge_results.explanation: The report discusses the trade-offs between memory savings and generation quality across multiple KV-cache optimization techniques (such as MQA, GQA, Quantized KV Cache, and MLA) in both the text descriptions of Section 3 and the structured comparison matrix in Section 4.

judge_results.criterion_text: Claims that KV-cache optimization has no impact on output quality
judge_results.explanation: The report evaluates multiple KV-cache optimization techniques and clearly indicates that several of them (such as Multi-Query Attention, INT4 Quantization, and Grouped-Query Attention) lead to moderate-to-high, negligible, or near-perfect impact on model output quality, rather than claiming no impact at all across the board. Thus, it avoids the pitfall described in the negative criterion.
